Scoped external review found participant, freshness, and lifecycle-state soundness gaps.

findings.summary: Non-participant sources can satisfy the approval threshold
findings.detail: Source validation checks allowed_sources but not policy.participants. With participants [subagent, external] and an additional configured human source, approvals from subagent and human reach min_independent=2 and emit the milestone even though external was the required assignment. This defeats the deterministic participant contract.

findings.summary: Verdict freshness does not validate the prepared target HEAD
findings.detail: Freshness compares only the in-scope digest, then reviewed_head is copied from the pending bundle. A commit affecting only out-of-scope paths after prepare leaves that digest unchanged, so the verdict is accepted and records an obsolete reviewed_head. Plan-review approvals are even less protected because they do not run this freshness check. Require the prepared target_head to match the accepted committed target before recording evidence.

findings.summary: review prepare does not reject an invalid reviewer lifecycle state
findings.detail: The plan explicitly requires review prepare to fail closed for an invalid reviewer state, but prepare loads policy and compiles immediately without calling the existing reviewer-state validator. It can therefore create authoritative assignments for plan-reviewer or code-reviewer outside their corresponding review state.

payload.reason: Scoped subagent review found plan-artifact drift coverage and CLI documentation gaps.

findings.summary: Undeclared plan drift is not detected for directory-scoped or deleted artifacts.
The documented review-reject exit codes omit the new validation failure.

payload.reason: Code review found that the generated CLI reference fix requires its generator and generator test in scope.
Dogfood found late plan redeclaration compiles implementation files into plan-review assignments; plan review must target only declared plan/spec artifacts.

Bundle-only Medium review approved the plan artifact-scope revision.
Unresolvable historical baseline must fall back to full-change

checklist.note: Unresolvable historical source baselines fail preparation instead of compiling to full-change as required by acceptance criterion 3.
A malformed or missing reviewed_head commit in an otherwise parseable historical event raises GitScopeError.

findings.summary: compile_review_contract calls is_ancestor directly for a recorded source baseline, so an unresolvable reviewed_head aborts review prepare. Historical event payloads do not guarantee that reviewed_head names an existing commit; such a baseline should be treated as unusable and fall back to the full-change assignment, matching the documented partial/stale/non-ancestor behavior.

payload.reason: Empty scoped assignment must not widen to full diff

checklist.note: Empty scoped assignments violate the exact scoped inspection contract.
The generated argv can expose out-of-scope changes.
Empty in-scope file lists are not handled safely.

findings.summary: scope_diff_argv emits `git diff <base>..<head> --` when the scoped file list is empty. Git interprets that as an unrestricted diff, so an assignment with no matching changes, such as a plan-only review after implementation commits, silently exposes the entire delta and contradicts its empty `inspection.files`. Return an explicitly empty inspection command or otherwise prevent dispatch from widening the target.

checklist.note: Frozen-contract and retry guarantees have correctness gaps.
The assigned delta is sufficient and remains within the declared target.
Two fail-open paths can approve evidence under state different from the frozen contract.
Failed-subset retry can become impossible when a retained source reported findings.
The new CLI and lifecycle are extensively documented.

findings.summary: Result import validates the lifecycle state and frozen result bindings but never verifies that current HEAD still equals the round's target_head. A commit made while runs are pending can therefore receive code_review_passed/READY_TO_MERGE from results that inspected the previous commit.

Round closure reloads live review governance instead of using the governance frozen into the contract. Changing governance while a round is pending can reduce required sources or disable distinct-family enforcement, allowing the old round to approve under requirements its reviewers were never issued against.

A failed-subset retry freezes all currently open finding IDs, including findings newly reported by a retained peer in the execution-failed round, and import requires the retrying source to dispose them. Peer verdicts are intentionally hidden and the retry prompt cannot reliably supply these records, so the documented retained-success retry path can reject every otherwise valid retry result.

checklist.note: Legacy events must remain readable without weakening their original review requirements.
The assigned target was sufficient and review remained within its delta and supporting context.
Static inspection, AST parsing, and diff checks passed; pytest could not start because the read-only environment provides no usable temporary directory.
An in-flight round created before these new frozen-governance fields were introduced can lose distinct-model-family enforcement after upgrade.
The concepts update accurately describes the intended behavior for newly created rounds.

findings.summary: Rounds started by the previous release have no require_distinct_model_families field, and the compatibility fold defaults it to false. After upgrading super-harness while such a round is pending, closure no longer applies the previously configured distinct-family requirement and can emit an approval from same-family receipts. This violates the plan's legacy-event compatibility requirement and weakens the merge gate; legacy rounds must recover the frozen requirement from their contract or fail closed instead of silently disabling it.

prior_findings.note: Import now resolves current HEAD and rejects it when it differs from the frozen target; terminal closure also suppresses milestones for a stale target.

New rounds persist required sources, minimum independence, and distinct-family policy in review_round_started, and automated closure consumes those derived frozen values instead of reloading live governance.

Failed-source retries now retain the original execution-failed round's open_finding_ids and compile the unchanged contract from the pre-round event prefix, so retained peer findings are not imposed on the retrying source.
